channel.title: UK Political Party Jobs
channel.description: Job listings for Political Parties roles, scraped by Pol-Intel.

item.title: Regional Organiser: South East, East of England & London (PT)
item.description: It is an exciting time for the Co-operative Party with our largest ever membership and a strong team of staff, activists and representatives. Our team of Regional Organisers work locally and regionally to grow and develop our membership.
item.dc:creator: Co-operative Party

item.title: Campaigns Manager - St Albans constituency
item.description: Job Title: Campaigns Manager - St Albans constituency Employer : St Albans Liberal Democrats Responsible to: East of England Campaign Manager Salary : £36,000 Hours : Full time (37.5 hours per week) Tenure : Permanent Location : St Albans / Home based Closing date : 13:00 on Thursday 18th June Interviews : first and second round interviews likely to be held in person on Friday 3rd July and Thursday 9th July Job Description This is an exciting opportunity to join the St Albans Liberal Democrats at a pivotal moment for local and national politics. With three major elections expected over the next three years, we are looking for an ambitious and driven Campaign Manager to help lead one of the party’s most successful local parties through a defining period of growth and electoral success. St Albans is one of the Liberal Democrats’ strongest constituencies. In the 2024 General Election, Daisy Cooper was re-elected as MP for St Albans with almost 57% of the vote, and in the 2026 district elections, the local team successfully defended every district seat in the constituency. The years ahead will bring significant political change and opportunity. Following local government reorganisation, we expect the creation of a number of unitary authorities within Hertfordshire, leading to: 2027 – inaugural elections for new unitary councils 2028 – the first election for a Hertfordshire Mayor 2028/29 – the next General Election, with additional target parliamentary seats across the county This role offers the chance to play a central part in shaping and delivering winning campaigns across this crucial period. The successful candidate will lead campaigning activity in the St Albans constituency, developing and delivering a clear long-term electoral strategy while also overseeing strong day-to-day campaign execution. Given the strength and experience of the local party, there will also be opportunities to support campaign development, training and capacity-building across Hertfordshire. We are looking for someone who combines strategic thinking with a practical, hands-on approach: a highly organised campaigner who can motivate volunteers, manage competing priorities and help deliver ambitious electoral outcomes. Why Join Us? Play a leading role in one of the Liberal Democrats’ key political regions Work alongside experienced campaigners and elected representatives Shape strategy across multiple high-profile elections Opportunity for professional growth and increased responsibility Be part of a motivated, ambitious and successful local team Potential for the role to become a higher-profile regional role over time Who are we looking for? We are looking for an ambitious and effective campaigner who understands what it takes to win competitive campaigns and inspire others to perform at their best. You may have developed your experience within the Liberal Democrats or in another fast-paced campaigning environment — such as a political party, advocacy organisation, membership body, trade union or charity. Above all, we are looking for someone with a proven ability to deliver results, manage competing priorities and thrive in high-pressure, target-driven environments. Key Tasks Lead and deliver professional, compliant and strategically focused election campaigns across the St Albans constituency. Work closely with the MP, candidates, councillors and local campaign teams to develop campaign plans, set clear objectives and drive progress against voter contact, fundraising and organisational targets. Support the effective selection and approval of candidates in line with party processes and standards. Help shape and implement a clear constituency-wide messaging strategy , ensuring consistent and effective communication across all local campaigns. Play a leading role in the planning, production and delivery of campaign literature, maintaining high standards of messaging, design and targeting. Support and coordinate local campaign teams across the constituency, ensuring activists and volunteers feel valued, motivated and well-supported and working with local teams to ensure that members and activists are regularly contacted personally to increase volunteer engagement and development . Build and strengthen a sustainable campaign infrastructure through volunteer recruitment, leadership development and local fundraising. Analyse campaign data a nd voter feedback to inform campaign decisions, targeting and strategic priorities. Work with local teams to deliver effective doorstep and direct voter communication programmes. Manage relationships with external commercial suppliers , including printers, to ensure cost-effective and high-quality campaign delivery, securing bulk-buy deals Design, organise and attend relevant meetings, campaign events and training sessions as required. Person Specification Essential Highly organised and self-motivated, with the ability to manage competing priorities and work independently to deliver agreed objectives.
item.dc:creator: Liberal Democrats

item.title: Commercial Manager
item.description: Responsible to: Head of Conferences & Events Salary: £38,000 to £43,800 per annum Tenure: Permanent Hours: Full time, 37.5 hours per week Location: Hybrid -LDHQ, Central London & WFH - plus travel for the Federal Conferences in the UK Purpose of job Responsibility for sales across all revenue streams – sponsorship, exhibition, fringe events and advertising for the two Federal Conferences. Additionally responsible for the overall marketing strategy across the commercial opportunities as well as the membership. Key Responsibilities Sourcing, negotiating and contracting all fringe space for the Party’s two annual federal conferences to maximise fringe revenue potential at each venue. Setting up price structures for each space Working with the conference venue and shell scheme contractor to draw up floorplans which will maximise exhibition revenue potential at each venue Responsible for securing exhibition bookings for the Party’s two annual federal conferences, through both commercial and charity organisations Identifying sponsorship and branding opportunities available at each conference then packaging, pricing, marketing and selling accordingly Responsible for all advertising opportunities available through the Party’s conference publications Maintaining existing client base to secure repeat business year on year, and identifying, targeting and developing new business via email, over the phone and through face to face meetings Lead contact for all exhibitors and sponsors throughout the year. Responsible for all invoicing, and ensuring clients have all information they need on time Keeping abreast of political and market changes and tailoring services and products to existing and potential exhibitors and sponsors accordingly Produce all commercial marketing materials. Responsible for collating all required documentation and information from clients in the lead up to conference (health and safety forms, client registration, etc.) Ensuring compliance with health and safety legislation in the exhibition hall and at all sponsored events Onsite at conference: the main point of contact for all clients and contractors during set up, running and breakdown of conference. Ensure the smooth running of the exhibition hall, VIP lounge and sponsored activities. Work with the volunteer team to ensure fringe events run smoothly and all commercial clients get the most out of conference. Maximise on-site opportunities to secure repeat business and new leads Attending venue site visits as needed and client meetings onsite at the venues as required Working closely with the Fundraising Department to share contacts, cross-sell and maximise revenues in both departments Responsible for the commercial section of the conference website. Ensure the website is up to date and marketing the commercial benefits of conference effectively Attending all Conference & Communications Committee meetings - ensuring calendar invites, agendas and minutes are distributed in a timely manner and aiding with information requests when required From time to time, any other duties to support the efforts of the Conference team and the Party as appropriate PERSON SPECIFICATION We are looking for a highly organised, highly-motivated and confident individual with a track record of delivering to organisational targets. They will have creative flair and the ability to think outside the box. This position would suit a tenacious negotiator with the drive to exceed expectations. Essential Skills and Experience At least 3 years’ experience in a similar role Proven track record of event income generation through multiple streams Strong negotiation skills Outstanding stakeholder management – managing relationships across all levels Excellent written communication skills – compiling marketing copy, proofreading etc. Excellent customer service ethos Sympathy with the aims and values of the Liberal Democrats How to apply All applicants must have the unrestricted, legal right to live and work in the UK. Please note that we are currently unable to offer visa sponsorship for this role. To submit an application, please email recruitment@libdems.org.uk, attaching: 1. A copy of your CV, including complete work history 2. A Covering Letter: no more than one page of A4 in length, indicating clearly how your experience and skills meet the criteria stated in the job and person specification 3. A completed diversity monitoring form 4. Please ensure we have the means to contact you by phone and email We will be reviewing applications as they come in on a rolling basis. We will not notify applicants if their application has been unsuccessful at the shortlisting stage. Please note: We will not be able to employ you if you are not eligible to work in the UK. We will not be able to obtain a work permit on your behalf. Applicants are encouraged to inform us if any reasonable adjustments are needed to be made during any part of the recruitment process. Please let us have details of your c
item.dc:creator: Liberal Democrats

item.title: Regional Capacity & Membership Officer (London)
item.description: Job Title: Regional Capacity & Membership Officer (London) Responsible to: London Campaign Manager Tenure: Fixed term contract until Friday 28th May 2027 Hours: 18.75 hours per week (0.5 FTE - 2.5 working days per week) Location: Home based, a location commutable around London region Salary: London Living Wage - £28,860 FTE (£14,430 pro rata for 18.75 hours per week). PURPOSE Politics in London - and across the country - stands at a crossroads. Londoners increasingly feel abandoned by Labour and the Conservatives, and populism is on the rise. There is a real opportunity for the Liberal Democrats in London to seize - to win in all sorts of communities across the capital and deliver for local people. But only with the right people and teams in place to seize it. The Capacity & Membership Officer will be at the heart of a team of staff and volunteers working to identify and empower new activists, grow our teams, and develop our local parties - with a particular focus on boroughs that currently do not have Lib Dem representation. It’s only by growing our footprint right across the capital that we can take on the dangerous forces of populism and put Londoners of all backgrounds first. Through a programme of phoning, mentoring, and developing single issue campaigns, the postholder will collaborate with the London regional party and local parties in our boroughs to find new activists and members - and crucially make sure they find a rewarding and impactful place in our teams. Main Responsibilities The successful candidate will play a key role in a team of staff and volunteers, whose primary focus is to grow our member and activist base - especially in our smallest local parties without elected Liberal Democrat representatives. They will identify and recruit new members and activists - through a systematic program of phoning prospects, identifying skills and interests, and integrating them into local parties so that they have a rewarding and impactful experience. They will ensure that any offers of help - online, on the doorstep or anywhere else - are acted upon, and will work to help local parties and other volunteers use our tech to engage with and track new prospects. Together with a team of staff and volunteers, they will help to develop single issue campaigns on key issues for Londoners and in line with Liberal Democrat values - with a particular focus on monitoring the success and uptake of these campaigns, and developing new member and activist prospects discovered through these campaigns. They will work to improve the new member and activist experience - helping smaller local parties to more effectively take on new offers of help, and ensuring that new recruits are not left to wither. They will work alongside the existing staff and volunteer teams to coordinate mentoring and training for new recruits. They will develop plans - including mentoring - to increase the diversity of our membership and activist base in London. Person Specification Essential 1. Sympathy with the aims and values of the Liberal Democrats. 2. Experience of working with teams of people - especially volunteers. 3. Ability to create a positive, welcoming and motivating climate for potential Liberal Democrat candidates and activists, from across the spectrum of our region’s diverse makeup, whether or not they are currently party members. 4. Good communication skills, both written and verbal. 5. Good IT skills, and the ability to learn new systems quickly. 6. The ability to work with, and motivate, volunteers and teams. 7. The ability and willingness to undertake sustained periods of phone calling to new prospects. 8. Ability to work without immediate supervision to deliver agreed objectives. 9. Able to work flexibly, including weekends and evenings. Desirable 1. Experience of developing or delivering issue-based campaigns - in politics or any other sphere. 2. Experience of mass phoning to achieve an objective - e.g. sales, recruitment or donations. 3. Experience of delivering training and/or mentoring. 4. Familiarity with Liberal Democrat systems like Connect and Lighthouse. How to apply: Please fully read through the requirements of the job before applying and take a few minutes to complete our Equality and Diversity Monitoring Forms then proceed with your application. A full CV of no more than two pages and including a telephone number and email address A covering letter (no more than one A4 page) setting out why you want the job and how your experience and skills are relevant to it To be considered for this position, please submit your application to recruitment@libdems.org.uk We will be reviewing applications as they come in and reserve the right to appoint prior to the closing date Please note: We will not be able to employ you if you are not eligible to work in the UK. We will not be able to obtain a work permit on your behalf. Applicants are encouraged to inform us if any reasonable adjustments are needed to be made during any part
item.dc:creator: Liberal Democrats

item.title: Parliamentary Researcher for David Green MSP (Caithness, Sutherland and Ross)
item.description: Salary: £33,000 per annum Hours: Full time, 37 hours per week Location: Scottish Parliament, Edinburgh Job Details David Green MSP is looking for a Parliamentary Researcher, based in their office in the Scottish Parliament. Experience of working in politics, public affairs, campaigns or similar environments is desirable, but not essential. Responsibilities: Draft speeches for parliamentary debates such as members’ business. Draft questions to be asked in the Chamber. Devise and submit freedom of information requests and written parliamentary questions. Analyse statistics and publications. Carry out research projects and write briefings. Help sort the MSP’s email inbox, tracking, drafting and responding to correspondence. Work with casework colleagues to identify opportunities to raise constituents’ issues in the Chamber. Arrange meetings and events, helping arrange the MSP’s diary around parliamentary business. Ensure compliance with data protection legislation and GDPR requirements. Draft press releases targeted at Caithness, Sutherland and Ross, building relationships with local media, and monitoring local media to maintain a good awareness of local issues. Growing the MSP’s social media presence and engagement, working as part of a team to keep constituents informed about how they are being represented at Parliament through engaging content. Requirements: Be curious and driven to uncover new and interesting information. Be a quick learner with the ability to multitask and work with accuracy within tight timescales. Be a proactive problem solver with the motivation and resilience to adapt and deal with challenges. Have analytical skills with the ability to identify and investigate trends while interpreting data. Have the creative flair and strategic thinking to produce influential work. Occasional travel and out-of-hours work may be required. How to Apply: For any questions about the role please contact: David.Green.msp@parliament.scot Please apply via this link: Job search . Upload your CV along with a supporting statement as a single document. Please note that the system does not allow you to upload your supporting statement separately. Your supporting statement should outline how you meet the requirements detailed above for this role. Applications that do not include a supporting statement may not be considered. Closing Date: Thursday 18 th June at 11:59pm
item.dc:creator: Liberal Democrats

item.title: Caseworker for Adam Harley MSP (Strathkelvin and Bearsden)
item.description: Salary: £29,335 - £36,012 (depending on experience) Contract: Permanent Hours: 37 hours per week Closing date: 16th June 2026 Location: Hybrid; based in the constituency office (Strathkelvin and Bearsden) Mondays and Fridays with the option of home working Tuesday – Thursday. Occasional attendance at Holyrood as required. Adam Harley MSP is recruiting a full-time Caseworker to join his team in the Strathkelvin and Bearsden constituency. This is a full-time role with the hours split between working from the constituency office on Mondays and Fridays and working from home and at times from Holyrood Tuesdays – Thursdays. This role involves supporting constituents with a wide range of issues and ensuring they receive effective representation and assistance. The successful candidate will work closely with the MSP and office team to manage casework, engage with local organisations, and support constituency priorities. This role offers opportunities for development and progression for the right candidate. Responsibilities Ensuring that every constituent who contacts the office gets a response and follow-up to their query. Supporting with keeping casework up to date and responding to public enquiries. Keeping cases up to date by tracking, logging and recognising when to escalate issues within team. Meeting constituents on a wide range of issues, including those of a sensitive nature and escalating accordingly. Advocating on constituents’ behalf with relevant bodies to resolve matters. Attending surgeries, producing reports and monitoring media trends on my behalf. Conducting basic research and surveys. Investigating patterns of enquiries and producing reports. Monitoring local and regional media and generating media coverage. Briefing me and drafting responses. Reviewing constituent records and documentation in line with casework GDPR requirements. Ensuring casework filing systems, both electronic and manual, are kept up-to-date in line with GDPR requirements. Requirements Have excellent organisational skills while being flexible in prioritising workloads during busy times. Have excellent communication skills, with accurate attention to detail and the ability to work effectively with colleagues. Be a self-starter, able to identify tasks that need completing. Be patient and sensitive while remaining objective and open-minded. Be comfortable being in a front-facing role explaining regulations such as GDPR to the general public. Be acutely aware of the responsibility of representing those unable to do so themselves in certain situations. Have a natural interest in helping people with a curiosity to probe for further information where required. To apply, please send a CV and a supporting statement outlining your suitability for the role to adam.harley.msp@parliament.scot with "Caseworker – Job Application” in the subject line. Closing date: 16th June 2026 at 11:59pm
item.dc:creator: Liberal Democrats

item.title: Supporter Care Manager
item.description: Supporter Care Manager | Green Party Careers Now Hiring Supporter Care Manager Lead the Supporter Care Team and oversee the experience people have when communicating with the Green Party, helping us grow towards even more victories in 2028. 💷 Salary £46,031 – £47,797 ⏰ Hours 35 per week 📍 Location Remote · London 📋 Contract Permanent 📅 Closing Date 18th Jun 2026 About This Role Are you passionate about making a real impact and driving change? Do you want to be at the forefront of ensuring the Green Party is ready to win even more MPs in the next election? After our success in winning four MPs at the last election, we are now gearing up for 2028, aiming to secure even more victories. Our membership and supporters are key to achieving this, and this role will oversee the experience people have when communicating with the party with their queries and support the wider National Engagement team with their planning and access. As the Supporter Care Manager, you will oversee the moments when our community need questions answering – from members checking their subscriptions, officers needing help navigating the database experience, or supporters with questions about our policy. You will also manage the telephone fundraising team, managing their campaigns by working with the Retention Manager and Field team. In this role, you will have the opportunity to build something truly special, working with people across the organisation to ensure the Green Party grows in line with its ambitions. Full Job Description For the best experience on mobile, view or download the full job description below. 📄 View Job Description (PDF) Ready to Apply? Join the National Engagement Team and lead our Supporter Care function as we build towards 2028. Apply Now Applications Close 11:00am · 18th June 2026 Interviews Week commencing 29th June 2026
item.dc:creator: Green Party of England and Wales

item.title: Campaign Manager (South East)
item.description: Responsible to : Head of Campaigns & Elections (UK) Salary: £36,073.36 Hours : Full-time (37.5 hours per week) Tenure: Permanent Location: Home-based, with travel in the South East and occasionally elsewhere Closing date: 5 pm Wednesday 17th June Purpose of the Job To deliver election success in priority areas across the South East, with a clear objective of putting “more gold on the map” – based on a programme of work jointly agreed between the Federal Party and South East Regional Party. This is a senior regional role focused on winning. You will work with MPs, candidates, councillors, volunteers and staff to build professional, disciplined and ambitious campaigns that convert strong local foundations into electoral success. Key responsibilities Lead and deliver winning campaigns in priority areas across the South East, directly managing key contests where required and ensuring campaigns are professional, compliant and strategically focused. Work closely with MPs, candidates, councillors and local party teams to run structured campaign planning meetings, set clear objectives and drive progress against voter contact, fundraising and organisational targets. Build sustainable and self-sufficient campaign teams in held seats by strengthening volunteer recruitment, leadership capacity and local fundraising. Develop campaign-ready infrastructure in emerging priority seats, including clear strategy, messaging frameworks, data-led targeting and measurable growth plans. Support and develop local organisers and campaign staff through mentoring, training, and, where agreed, line management, helping to grow a confident and capable regional team. Embed best practice in data, digital campaigning, literature production and compliance across the region. Person specification We are looking for a strategic campaigner who understands what it takes to win competitive elections and can help others raise their game. You may have developed your experience within a political party, an advocacy organisation, a membership body, a trade union, a charity, or a comparable campaigning environment. What matters most is a track record of delivering results in high-pressure, target-driven campaigns. You will demonstrate: Experience of managing or playing a senior role in complex campaigns with measurable outcomes. A strong understanding of political messaging and how to translate strategy into compelling communications across print, digital and direct voter contact. The ability to analyse data and voter insight to inform targeting, messaging and resource allocation. Strong leadership skills, with experience motivating volunteers or staff, building effective teams and building positive relationships with stakeholders. Sound political judgement and an instinct for what resonates with voters. The confidence to challenge constructively, set high standards and hold teams accountable for delivery. The ability to work independently across a large region, prioritising effectively and responding calmly to unexpected developments. You will be someone who combines strategic thinking with practical delivery, and who enjoys working with teams at different stages of development — from well-established strongholds to emerging growth areas. Essential Skills and Experience Significant experience in campaign management or comparable project leadership in a political or advocacy context. Strong written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to shape persuasive messaging. Excellent organisational and planning skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously. Experience working with volunteers and/or distributed teams. Confidence using data, CRM systems and digital campaign tools to support targeting and reporting. The ability to operate effectively across a large geographic area, with a full clean driving licence and access to a car. Sympathy with the aims and values of the Liberal Democrats. Desirable Skills and Experience Experience of managing staff, including remote management. Experience of electoral campaigns. Experience of fundraising strategy and delivery. Experience using tools such as Fleet, Lib Dem Connect, Lighthouse, Affinity Publisher (or similar design software), and Typeform — or the ability to learn new systems quickly. Experience designing or delivering training and mentoring programmes. How to apply: Please read the job requirements carefully before applying, complete our Equality and Diversity Monitoring Forms, and then proceed with your application. A full CV of no more than two pages, including a telephone number and email address A covering letter (no more than one A4 page) setting out why you want the job and how your experience and skills are relevant to it To be considered for this position, please submit your application to recruitment@libdems.org.uk . We will review applications as they come in and reserve the right to appoint before the closing date. Please note: We will not be able to employ
item.dc:creator: Liberal Democrats

item.title: Campaigns Manager for Eastbourne
item.description: Employer : Eastbourne Liberal Democrats Line Manager: South East Regional Campaign Manager Salary : £32,000 - £37,000, dependant on experience Hours : Full-time (37.5 hours per week) Tenure : 1-year fixed-term contract (with a view to making permanent, pending review in June 2027) Location : Hybrid: 100 Seaside Road, Eastbourne, BN21 3PF and home-based Closing date : Sunday 5th July 2026 at 11.59 pm Interviews : w/c 20th July 2026 JOB DESCRIPTION This is an exciting opportunity to join the Eastbourne Liberal Democrats at a pivotal moment for local and national politics. With significant elections expected over the next three years, we are looking for an ambitious and driven Campaign Manager to help lead one of the party’s most successful local parties through a defining period of growth. Eastbourne is one of the Liberal Democrats’ strongest constituencies. In the 2024 General Election, Josh Babarinde OBE was elected as MP for Eastbourne with 23,742 votes, winning 52% of the vote and a 26.8% lead. Josh won the seat from the Conservatives. Josh was elected President of the Liberal Democrats in November 2025, taking office in January 2026. In the East Sussex County Council elections in May 2026, the Liberal Democrats won seven of the nine Wards in the constituency. They won the Meads Ward from the Greens and only just missed taking the Ratton Ward from the Conservatives, losing by just forty votes. Reform took the Sovereign Ward from the conservatives. The political landscape in Eastbourne, as elsewhere in the country, has changed significantly in the last twelve months, posing significant political challenges. We are expecting elections to the new One East Sussex Unitary Authority in May 2027. A government decision on the final shape of the new Unitary Authority is expected before the Summer Recess. This role offers the chance to play a central part in shaping and delivering winning campaigns across this crucial period. The successful candidate will lead campaigning activity in the Eastbourne constituency, developing and delivering a clear long-term electoral strategy while also overseeing strong day-to-day campaign execution. We are looking for someone who combines strategic thinking with a practical, hands-on approach: a highly organised campaigner who can motivate volunteers, manage competing priorities and help deliver ambitious electoral outcomes. Why Join Us? Play a leading role in one of the Liberal Democrats’ key political constituencies Work alongside experienced campaigners and elected representatives Shape strategy across multiple high-profile elections Opportunity for professional growth and increased responsibility Be part of a motivated, ambitious and successful local team Potential for the role to become a higher-profile regional role over time Who are we looking for? We are looking for an ambitious and effective campaigner who understands what it takes to win competitive campaigns and inspire others to perform at their best. You may have developed your experience within the Liberal Democrats or in another fast-paced campaigning environment — such as a political party, advocacy organisation, membership body, trade union or charity. Above all, we are looking for someone with a proven ability to deliver results, manage competing priorities and thrive in high-pressure, target-driven environments. Key Tasks Lead and deliver professional, compliant and strategically focused election campaigns across the Eastbourne constituency. Work closely with the MP, candidates, councillors and local campaign teams to develop campaign plans, set clear objectives and drive progress against voter contact, fundraising and organisational targets. Working with the local party Chair, support the effective selection and approval of candidates in line with party processes and standards. Help shape and implement a clear constituency-wide messaging strategy , ensuring consistent and effective communication across all local campaigns. Lead the planning, production and delivery of campaign literature, maintaining high standards of messaging, design and targeting. Support and coordinate local campaign teams across the constituency, ensuring activists and volunteers feel valued, motivated and well-supported. Work with local teams to ensure that effective relationships are established with members and activists, and to increase engagement and development . Build and strengthen a sustainable campaign infrastructure through volunteer recruitment, leadership development and local fundraising. Analyse campaign data and voter feedback to inform campaign decisions, targeting and strategic priorities. Work with local teams to deliver effective doorstep and direct voter communication programmes, setting and ensuring ambitious literature delivery and canvassing targets are met Manage relationships with external commercial suppliers , including printers, to ensure cost-effective and high-quality campaign delivery, securing bulk-buy deals. Design, organise and attend relevant meetings, campaign events and training sessions as required. Person Specification Essential Highly organised and self-motivated, with the ability to manage competing priorities and work independently to deliver agreed objectives. Experience in planning and delivering complex projects, campaigns or workstreams to deadline. Strong stakeholder management skills, with experience of working effectively with senior colleagues or other key decision-makers. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to communicate persuasively and accurately with a range of audiences. Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to motivate, influence and inspire candidates, volunteers and campaign teams. Experience in volunteer engagement, recruitment or team leadership. An enthusiasm for coaching and developing the skills and confidence of others. Good strategic judgement and the ability to translate plans into effective delivery. Confident IT and digital communication skills, including Microsoft Office, email systems and social media platforms. A good understanding of messaging and graphic design in the political context. Sympathy with the aims and values of the Liberal Democrats. Desirable Experience of working with elected representatives and/or volunteer-led organisations Experience of using Liberal Democrat campaign systems, including Connect, Lighthouse and Affinity Publisher. Experience in delivering training or facilitating workshops. An understanding of political messaging, targeting and campaign design. Full UK driving licence and access to a vehicle. Workplace Flexible: your working week will be split between being in the local party office in Eastbourne and working from home. You may also visit local parties around East Sussex and may be expected to attend occasional staff meetings in London. Key stakeholders You will formally report to the South East Regional Campaign Manager. You will work closely with the Chair of the Eastbourne Liberal Democrats (the employer), Josh Babarinde, the MP for Eastbourne, the principal council leader, the local campaign team and the relevant campaign oversight and planning groups. How to apply: Please read the job description to learn more about the role before applying, and take a few minutes to complete our Equality and Diversity Monitoring Forms (the information from which is confidential and not shared with the panel). Then proceed with your application. Please submit an application form here to apply: https://bit.ly/EBcampaignmanager Please note: We will not be able to employ you if you are not eligible to work in the UK. We will not be able to obtain a work permit on your behalf. Applicants are encouraged to inform us if any reasonable adjustments are needed at any point in the recruitment process. Please let us have details of your two referees, including at least one related to your campaigning experience. If you would prefer us not to contact them until a later stage of the selection process, please let us know. We will not notify applicants if their application has been unsuccessful at the shortlisting stage. The Party believes it is essential to foster equity, equality, diversity, and inclusion within our workforce. We want our employees to thrive in an environment where everyone is welcome and supported to achieve their potential. It is important to celebrate what makes us unique and to feel valued, appreciated, and free to be who we are.
item.dc:creator: Liberal Democrats
item.polintel:location: Hybrid: 100 Seaside Road, Eastbourne, BN21 3PF and home-based

item.title: Membership Services Executive
item.description: Job Title: Membership Services Executive Responsible to: Membership Manager Salary: £28,860 Hours: Full-time 37.5 Tenure: Permanent Location: This role is office-based from our main Headquarters at 66 Buckingham Gate, London, SW1E 6AU. 🎉 Join the Party and Let's Make Politics Fun! 🎈 Hey there, superstar! Are you ready for an electrifying opportunity with the UK's third-largest political party? Our Membership Services team is where the action happens, and we want YOU to be the life of the party! In this close-knit, passionate crew, you'll be the shining star as the official face and voice of our Party. From membership magic to Party puzzle-solving, you'll be the go-to guru, ensuring our members and the public have a brilliant membership experience, all delivered with a burst of enthusiasm and efficiency. 🚀 What's in it for You? 🌟 Get ready to dive deep into the inner workings of our Party and become an essential part of our dynamic team at this pivotal moment in our Party's epic journey. You'll be the lead dancer in exciting projects and initiatives, with a support team that's as solid as a rock. If you thrive in an environment that lets you shine, grow, and deliver top-notch customer service, then we can't wait to groove with you! 🎶 Key Responsibilities: 🌟 You'll be the first point of contact for all the telephone, email, and written excitement coming our way. Your superpower is ensuring that queries get answered in a flash, all while having a blast! You'll be the maestro of membership renewals, new sign-ups, and updates, mainly through email but sometimes with a little telephone magic and even some snail mail action. Your groove will be using our Customer Relationship Management (CRM) database to keep our membership records in perfect harmony. As the party's front-line hero, you'll build solid connections with other departments and make sure everyone – our teams and our members – gets what they need. When a query needs an extra dose of sparkle, you'll ensure it's taken to the next level promptly and collaborate with the point person to make the magic happen. But that's not all – you'll also get trained to be a Fire Warden and First Aider, and you might even become the Office Manager extraordinaire when duty calls. You might even get to be a surprise guest at other teams' parties, helping out with exciting projects and jumping into spontaneous fun! 🚀 The Perfect Dance Partner: 🌟 Essential Skills and Experience: You've got some customer service experience in your pocket, whether it's from in-person or call centre gigs. Multitasking is your jam, and you've got a smile that can light up a room. Your communication skills, both written and spoken, are the stars of the show. Organisation and administration? You're like a Swiss watch – precise and on point! You live by the "Customer First" motto, and you're confident with IT, ready to learn and grow with full support and training. Being part of a tight-knit team? You thrive on it, and you're a self-starter who can manage your workload with ease. Independence is your middle name! Desirable Skills and Experience: If you've got experience working with customer data and databases, that's a bonus. We groove with Salesforce for our CRM. Any previous involvement in a membership organisation, charity, or non-profit world would be a plus, but it's not a deal-breaker. Oh, and it's a given that you're familiar with and support the values of our Party – after all, it's the heart and soul of this fantastic political bash! 🎉 Let's Get the Party Started! 🎈 If you're ready to bring the fun, charm, and charisma to our high-energy team and make this campaign a blockbuster, we're waiting to hear from you! Don't wait in the wings – send us your application and let's rock the political scene together! 💃🕺✨🎉 And now the important bits. Please read through this Job Description fully to learn more about the role before applying and take a few minutes to complete our Equality and Diversity Monitoring Form here: https://digitallibdems.typeform.com/to/uvRCaZ2K . Then proceed with your application. You should include your full, current CV of no more than two pages, which includes your telephone number and email address, so we can get in touch with you. Please also include a short covering letter (no more than one side of A4) explaining why you would like to work for us and why your skills and experience are the perfect fit! This should be sent by email to recruitment@libdems.org.uk . You can also use this email address if you have any questions. Our policy is to review applications as they come in so you’re encouraged to register your interest as soon as you can. If you require any additional support or feel reasonable adjustments are needed as part of the recruitment process, please let us know. Please note: We will not be able to employ you if you are not eligible to work in the UK. We will not be able to obtain a work permit on your behalf. Due to the anticipated volume of applications, we
item.dc:creator: Liberal Democrats

item.title: Community Outreach Officer (Greater Manchester)
item.description: Community Outreach Officer (Greater Manchester) | Green Party Careers Now Hiring Community Outreach Officer (Greater Manchester) Engage communities across Greater Manchester’s ten boroughs, build relationships and mobilise volunteers at the heart of a high-stakes period of summer campaigning. 💷 Salary £29,858 – £32,192 ⏰ Hours 35 per week 📍 Location Greater Manchester · Hybrid 📋 Contract Fixed Term to 14th Aug 2026 📅 Closing Date 21st Jun 2026 About This Role The Green Party’s campaign team is seeking a Community Outreach Officer to boost our efforts to engage communities across the city-region who are too often overlooked by mainstream politics. This is a two-month assignment that is a hands-on, fast-paced role at the heart of a high-stakes period of summer campaigning, working directly with the regional Campaign Manager to build relationships, mobilise volunteers, and ensure our message reaches every corner of Greater Manchester’s ten boroughs. Applicants for this role would need to be available immediately. The Green Party is committed to building a team that reflects the diversity of Greater Manchester. We know that people from working-class backgrounds, Global Majority backgrounds, refugees and migrants and people without traditional political experience are particularly under-represented in politics and political campaigning — and we want that to change. If you don’t have a conventional CV or background in politics, but you have experience connecting with people, organising in your community, or building trust where it’s needed most, we want to hear from you. Lived experience and community knowledge are as valuable to us as formal qualifications. If you’re unsure whether you meet every point in the person specification, please apply anyway — we’d rather hear from you. You’ll support the development and delivery of outreach plans across Bolton, Bury, Manchester, Oldham, Rochdale, Salford, Stockport, Tameside, Trafford and Wigan; build relationships with community organisations, faith groups, cultural associations and local networks; recruit and support volunteers; and help ensure campaign materials are accessible and relevant to different communities. Full Job Description For the best experience on mobile, view or download the full job description below. 📄 View Job Description (PDF) Ready to Apply? Join the Greater Manchester campaign team and help us reach communities across all ten boroughs this summer. Apply Now Applications Close 23:45 · 21st June 2026 Interviews Week commencing 22nd June 2026
item.dc:creator: Green Party of England and Wales

item.title: Campaigns Organiser (Glastonbury & Somerton)
item.description: Job Title: Campaigns Organiser (Glastonbury & Somerton) Salary range: £27,000 Location: Glastonbury and Somerton Lib Dems HQ Days: Full time. It is expected that the Campaigns Organiser will work to assist evening and weekend campaign activities by agreement Tenure: Fixed term to June 2027 with potential to extend Responsible to: The designated Regional Campaigns Manager Closing date: Monday 29 th June 4pm Purpose of the job: Do you want to make the world around you a better place to live? Do you want to see your values making a real difference in peoples’ lives? Do you want to work with Glastonbury and Somerton’s Liberal Democrat MP and support the Lib Dem Somerset council? If you answered ‘yes’ to any of these questions, this role may be the perfect opportunity for you! Glastonbury and Somerton Liberal Democrats are seeking an enthusiastic and self-motivated person who can play a key role in building volunteer infrastructure, creating leaflets and organising doorstep activity – to support our fast-paced campaign. This job is pivotal to our success in the May 2027 Local Elections and getting Sarah Dyke re-elected. As Campaigns Organiser, you will be communicating with volunteers about upcoming campaign activities, recruiting and triaging new volunteers, designing and arranging distribution for campaign leaflets, coordinating constituency door knocking efforts, and working together with respective campaign teams to steer our campaigning efforts. Each of these tasks will require strong communication skills, a welcoming attitude, and the confidence to work with and engage volunteers. The successful candidate will be highly motivated by hitting targets, and proactive in keeping the pace of the campaign, getting stuck in on a range of different tasks and getting things done. Key Roles and Responsibilities: Lead the volunteer recruitment and capacity building/retainment processes in Glastonbury and Somerton to ensure we have a motivated and well-trained volunteer network of deliverers, canvassers and clerical helpers. Contribute to the literature schedule planning and provide key input to copy editing, design, proofing and approval, and print and distribution of regular artwork leaflets, direct mail letters and emails to members, supporters and voters. Campaign budgeting and financial management in liaison with the Treasurer/Election Agents. Plan and coordinate member and volunteer training, volunteer and capacity-building action days and constituency events. Designing and delivering an ongoing programme of communication to champion Liberal Democrat policy and achievements across Somerset. Coordinate and support membership recruitment and engagement strategy. Person specification Essential Ability to communicate effectively with and maintain the confidence of a wide variety of stakeholders. A willingness to recruit, mobilise and manage volunteers effectively. Demonstrate a "can-do" attitude and resilience in pressured/difficult situations. Understanding and experience of election campaigns and management. Ability to motivate and manage a team of people and work effectively as a team member Ability to work unsupervised and effectively prioritise from a wide variety of tasks. Ability to meet deadlines. Good written and oral communication skills. Ability to delegate effectively. Sympathetic to Liberal Democrat aims and/or values Desirable Knowledge of the local area, its politics and local communities. Ability to produce effective political literature. Good digital skills and willingness to learn new software/apps and media: social media, ability to update web sites, ability to use a variety of specialised software e.g. desktop publishing, video subtitling. Ability to spot issues with campaigning potential. Ability to work with the local media and generate positive coverage for the party and its representatives. Possession of driving licence and access to a vehicle, given the geographical nature of the Constituency. Starting date: ASAP. How to apply: ● A full CV and including contact information ● A covering letter of no more than two pages setting out why you want the job and how your experience and skills are relevant to it. To be considered for this position, please submit your application to Billy Thompson at billy.thompson@libdems.org.uk (Campaigns Manager Western Counties) We will be reviewing applications as they come in and reserve the right to appoint prior to the closing date. Please note: We will not be able to employ you if you are not eligible to work in the UK. We will not be able to obtain a work permit on your behalf. Applicants are encouraged to inform us if any reasonable adjustments are needed to be made during any part of the recruitment process. Please let us have details of your two referees including one from your current employer – if you would prefer us not to contact them until a later stage of the selection process, please let us know. The Party believes it is essential to foster equity,

item.title: Campaign & Volunteer Organiser
item.description: Job Title : Campaign & Volunteer Organiser Working For : Witney Liberal Democrats Location : Witney, Oxfordshire Salary : £28,000-£30,000 per annum pro-rata, depending on experience Length of Contract : Permanent (subject to the successful completion of a 3-month probation period). Job Details Hours : Full time including some weekend and evening work. Flexible and part time hours (minimum of 3 days per week) may be considered. Overview Charlie Maynard MP and the Witney Liberal Democrats are seeking an enthusiastic, politically astute and self-motivated person to plan and organise campaign activity and manage a team of volunteers. As Campaigns Organiser, you will be communicating with volunteers about upcoming campaign activities, phoning to recruit and activate new volunteers, designing and arranging distribution of campaign leaflets, coordinating constituency social media and door-knocking efforts. This requires strong communication skills and the ability to inspire and support volunteers. The successful candidate will be highly motivated, able to work in fast paced environment and be solution oriented. Job Description : Take overall responsibility for developing and delivering the constituency campaign plan to the agreed budget; working with the local party executive and campaign committee, Charlie Maynard MP and local, regional and HQ campaign staff. Organise volunteers and local party workers by: Recruiting, training, managing and mobilising members and volunteers Leading volunteer teams on campaigning activities Teaching volunteers to use the online database (Connect) effectively and entering data yourself. Ensure the delivery of a programme of social media, doorstep and telephone voter contact. Oversee and deliver the production, printing and distribution of literature. Taking charge of constituency emails, phone banking and updating of the Local Party Website. Assist with fundraising campaigns. Essential Criteria: ● Full UK driving licence and access to a vehicle. ● Ability to communicate with a wide variety of stakeholders. ● Ability to recruit, mobilise and manage volunteers. ● Demonstrate a "can-do" attitude and resilience in pressured/difficult situations. ● Ability to meet deadlines. ● Ability to work independently, with exceptional personal organisation. ● Sympathetic to Liberal Democrat aims and values. Desirable criteria: ● Knowledge of the local area. ● Understanding and experience of election campaigns. ● Good digital skills and experience of Liberal Democrat software and databases (e.g. Affinity Publisher & Connect) – training will be provided as needed. Closing Date : 22 June 2026 Interview/Start Dates We will aim to interview as soon as possible after the closing date. Start date: As soon as possible. Application Details Please submit: A full CV of no more than two pages, with full employment history, and including a telephone number and email address. A covering letter (no more than one A4 page) setting out why you want the job and how your experience and skills are relevant to it. Details of two referees, including one from your current or most recent employer (if applicable). Please send to: Paul Marsh - pjssj.marsh@btinternet.com - with the subject line: ‘Application - Campaign Organiser’. Website : https://www.oxonld.uk/witney-west-oxfordshire
item.dc:creator: Liberal Democrats

item.title: Constituency Support Manager
item.description: Job Title : Constituency Support Manager Working For : Charlie Maynard MP (Witney) Location : Witney, Oxfordshire Salary : In line with IPSA payscales (MP_staff_payranges_2026-27.pdf) Length of Contract : Permanent (subject to the successful completion of a 3-month probation period). Job Details Charlie Maynard MP for Witney is seeking a Constituency Support Manager for his busy constituency office. The ideal candidate will be a highly-motivated self-starter, adept at working with people to solve problems, with strong political instincts. The role is designed to be full time with a split contract. The majority will be working for Charlie Maynard MP, with some work for the Witney Liberal Democrats. Hours : Flexible. Applicants looking to work both full and part time (a minimum of three days per week) will be considered. Please specify the number of days per week you are available to work in your application. Key responsibilities: • Driving forward campaigns, at a variety of scales, to address local issues and deliver solutions for residents. • Organising and attending community meetings and taking forward actions arising from these. • Engaging and maintaining positive relationships with key local stakeholders including councillors, community organisations, faith groups, local campaign groups, public service providers, local businesses and constituents. • Monitoring local press, social media, and published data and reports about the constituency in order to keep up to date with developments in the local area and identify issues of concern to residents. • Managing Charlie’s diary for days he is in the constituency (working with his Parliamentary Assistant, who manages the diary in Westminster). • Working with the team’s communications officer to identify and manage topics for press releases and social media content. • Working with members of the team in Westminster and the Caseworkers to help ensure a joined-up approach to communicating and finding solutions to, challenges in the constituency. • Updating the MP and Local Party Website. Required skills • An ability to work with people to achieve results. • An understanding of the role of an MP as a constituency representative and champion. • An understanding of politics and strong political judgement. • Experience in setting up and running local campaigns. • Ability to manage in a fast-paced work environment and juggle competing priorities. • Supportive of the aims and values of the Liberal Democrats. Upon appointment you will be required to comply with the Baseline Personnel Security Standard, undertaken by the Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO). See Members’ Staff Verification Office (MSVO) page for further info. MPs generally pay staff in accordance with IPSA guidelines . Closing Date : 21 June 2026 Interview/Start Dates First round interviews will be held the week commencing 29th June. Start date is negotiable (as soon as possible). Application Details Please send a one page covering letter, together with your CV of no more than two pages, outlining your suitability for the position (and including the number of days per week you are available to work) to Charlie.Maynard.MP@Parliament.uk with the subject heading "Constituency Support Manager". Website : https://www.oxonld.uk/witney-west-oxfordshire
item.dc:creator: Liberal Democrats

item.title: Transformational Giving Manager
item.description: Transformational Giving Manager | Green Party Careers Now Hiring Transformational Giving Manager Lead the charge on growing the Green Party’s transformational gifts from individuals, helping ensure we are fully prepared for the next General Election. 💷 Salary £45,173 – £49,630 ⏰ Hours 35 per week 📍 Location Remote · London 📋 Contract Permanent 📅 Closing Date 3rd Jul 2026 About This Role Are you passionate about making a real impact and driving change? Do you want to be at the forefront of ensuring the Green Party is ready to win at least 30 MPs in the next election? After our success in winning four MPs at the last election, we are now gearing up for 2028, aiming to secure even more victories. To achieve this, we need to significantly boost our fundraising efforts, particularly through major gifts. As the Transformational Giving Manager, you will play a pivotal role in growing the Green Party’s transformational gifts from individuals. This is the first of this type of position in the Party, and you will be leading the charge to ensure we are fully prepared for the next General Election. You will be responsible for national-level income and supporting key constituencies in their work with donors. You’ll build and strengthen relationships with donors, connect them with the projects they care about, and ensure high-quality stewardship across the organisation. You’ll launch and grow a new Giving Circle, develop our newly founded Business Network, and evolve our donor scrutiny and thanking processes to deliver an excellent donor experience. In this role, you will have the opportunity to build trusting relationships, communicate effectively, and work both independently and as part of a dynamic team. Full Job Description For the best experience on mobile, view or download the full job description below. 📄 View Job Description (PDF) Ready to Apply? Join the National Engagement Team and lead the Green Party’s major gifts fundraising as we build towards 2028. Apply Now Applications Close 10:00am · 3rd July 2026 Interviews Online · w/c 13th July 2026

item.title: Assistant Graphic Design Officer
item.description: Assistant Graphic Design Officer | Green Party Careers Now Hiring Assistant Graphic Design Officer Join the Communications Team and support the delivery of high-quality visual content across the Party’s campaigns, social media, publications and events. 💷 Salary £29,858 – £32,192 FTE ⏰ Hours 28 per week · 4 days 📍 Location Remote · London 📋 Contract Permanent 📅 Closing Date 3rd Jul 2026 About This Role As the Green Party continues to grow, strong and consistent visual communication is increasingly critical to how we engage supporters, communicate campaigns, and present the Party across digital and print platforms. We’re seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Assistant Graphic Design Officer to join the Communications Team and support the delivery of high-quality visual content across the Party’s campaigns, social media, publications and events. Working closely with the Senior Design and Publications Officer and wider communications team, you will assist in producing engaging and accessible design outputs across print and digital platforms. You will play a key role in maintaining brand consistency, supporting reactive campaign work, and contributing to the continued development of the Party’s visual identity. This role is ideal for someone developing their design career, who is proactive, organised, collaborative, and eager to learn within a fast-paced political and campaigning environment. Please include a portfolio or example of recent work as part of your application. Join the Communications Team and help shape the Green Party’s visual identity. Remember to include a portfolio or example of recent work with your application. Apply Now Applications Close 10:00am · 3rd July 2026 Interviews Online · w/c 13th July 2026

item.title: Edinburgh Campaign Organiser (2 posts)
item.description: Job Title : Edinburgh Campaign Organiser (2 posts) Working For : Scottish Liberal Democrats Location : 4 Clifton Terrace, Edinburgh, EH12 5DR Salary : £28,000 pa Length of Contract : Fixed term until May 2027, with possibility of extension. Job Details The Scottish Liberal Democrats have a long history of success in Edinburgh – gaining a second Holyrood seat in the city this year. We are now looking for two dynamic campaign organisers to be a key part of our team, supporting our campaign activities, members, volunteers, and elected representatives in the run up to the city council elections in May 2027 and beyond. The campaign organisers will be the lynchpin of our campaigns in the area, helping us to deliver success as the party aims to gain councillors and lay the groundwork for further success at the next UK General Election. This is a great opportunity to take on a high-profile campaigning role with some of the most ambitious local parties in Scotland. Job Description We are recruiting two organisers, and there will be flexibility in the exact split of responsibilities between the two successful candidates. Key roles and responsibilities include: • Taking overall responsibility for delivering the agreed citywide campaign plan. • Building campaigning and organisational capacity – including recruiting members, donors, deliverers and poster sites. • Building and managing a team of volunteers who oversee all aspects of capacity building and campaigning. • Designing and delivering an ongoing programme of doorstep communication with voters. • Assisting with the design, production, print, and coordination of the delivery of campaign literature. • Maintaining relevant databases e.g. deliverers, donors, poster sites etc. • Organising events e.g. action days, social events, etc. • Maintaining and updating social media, website and other digital infrastructure. • Supporting fundraising and assisting with fundraising campaigns and events as required. • Attending relevant conferences, training and other meetings as required. Personal Specification The following criteria are considered essential: • Exceptional personal organisation. • Ability to multi-task. • Strong communication and interpersonal skills. • Ability to lead, mobilise and manage volunteers. • Excellent written communication skills with a high level of accuracy and attention to detail. • Ability to work independently with limited supervision, self-motivating and able to manage own workload. • Familiarity with current issues within UK politics and sympathy with the values and principles of the Liberal Democrats. The following criteria are desirable: • Experience in delivering effective Liberal Democrat campaigns. • Experience of Liberal Democrat software and databases (e.g. Affinity Publisher, NationBuilder & Connect) • Understanding of GDPR, data protection and regulations relevant to political campaigning • Full UK driving licence and access to a vehicle. The post is intended to be full time, but we will consider applications for a part time role Closing Date : Monday 6th July at 9am. Interview/Start Dates Interviews will be held on Wednesday 15 th or Thursday 16th July. We are looking to appoint someone to the role as soon as possible and potentially before the closing date. Application Details To apply for this role, please submit your CV (no more than two pages) with a covering letter (no more than one page) outlining your reasons for applying and detailing your relevant skills to Paul Trollope, CEO and Head of Campaigns, at paul.trollope@scotlibdems.org.uk Website : www.edinburghlibdems.org
item.dc:creator: Liberal Democrats

item.title: Senior Communications Officer
item.description: Job Title: Senior Communications Officer Responsible to: Head of Media Salary: from £35,000 Hours: Full Time Tenure: Permanent Location: LDHQ, Westminster, London Job Specification This is an exciting opportunity to join the Liberal Democrat media team, supporting the party’s record 72 Liberal Democrat MPs. The Senior Communications Officer will play a critical, leading role in shaping and delivering the Liberal Democrats' national media output. Reporting to the Head of Media, the role holder will be responsible for developing and implementing proactive communication plans that support the party’s spokespeople and peers in securing high-impact, positive national coverage. The candidate will be a resilient, autonomous, and ambitious communications professional looking for a chance to significantly influence political news and debate. You will have a record of building and maintaining a strong network of contacts with leading journalists across the national media and an expert understanding of the modern media landscape, including the interplay between traditional platforms and social media. You must demonstrate exceptional written and verbal communication skills and the ability to rapidly translate complex policy issues into compelling narratives. The ideal candidate will have an excellent understanding of UK politics, the opportunities facing the Liberal Democrats in the current political landscape, and the methods the party can employ to leverage its larger presence in Parliament for maximum impact across a range of channels. Key Responsibilities Lead the development and execution of proactive communications plans to secure high-impact coverage promoting the party’s work and national campaign priorities. Act as an expert adviser to spokespeople and their offices, identifying and creating opportunities to generate influential national media coverage across print and broadcast platforms. Work closely with senior management to assist in the delivery of crisis communications during the normal course of duties, managing complex narratives and mitigating media risks. Drive the integration of media and digital outputs, generating creative ideas for content to get our message across and ensuring media stories are optimised for social channels. Build and maintain relationships with senior network broadcast journalists to secure agenda-setting interviews and news packages. Proactively spot emerging trends and risks, using strategic planning to maintain and update the communications grid and ensure consistent, high-quality coverage. Contributing to the overall goals of and professional development within the Liberal Democrat media team, including supporting more junior colleagues. Take part in the media team’s out-of-hours duty system, including handling breaking news situations and providing senior handling advice when required. Undertake any other ad hoc duties as required to support senior leadership and wider team goals and responsibilities. How to apply All applicants must have the unrestricted, legal right to live and work in the UK. Please note that we are currently unable to offer visa sponsorship for this role. To submit an application, please email recruitment@libdems.org.uk attaching: 1. A copy of your CV, including complete work history 2. A Covering Letter: no more than one page of A4 in length, indicating clearly how your experience and skills meet the criteria stated in the job and person specification 3. A completed diversity monitoring form 4. Please ensure we have the means to contact you by phone and email We will be reviewing applications as they come in on a rolling basis. We will not notify applicants if their application has been unsuccessful at the shortlisting stage. Please note: We will not be able to employ you if you are not eligible to work in the UK. We will not be able to obtain a work permit on your behalf. Applicants are encouraged to inform us if any reasonable adjustments are needed to be made during any part of the recruitment process. Please let us have details of your current salary and two referees including one from your current employer – if you would prefer us not to contact them until a later stage of the selection process, please let us know. We will not notify applicants if their application has been unsuccessful at the shortlisting stage. The Party believes it is essential to foster equity, equality, diversion and inclusion within our workforce. We want our employees to thrive in an environment where everyone is welcome and supported to achieve their potential. It is important to celebrate what makes us unique and that you feel valued, appreciated and free to be who you are.
